---
title: "Introduction to Physical AI"
subtitle: "Course Website Template"
---

Instructor: Yonghyeon Lee

## Course Overview
This course offers an introductory survey of the core ideas underlying Physical AI. 
Topics include the fundamental components of embodied AI systems, 
such as perception, planning, control, and both learning-based and physics-based approaches. 
Emphasis is placed on conceptual understanding rather than mathematical detail. 
The course aims to help students understand the broader landscape of Physical AI, 
recognize its main technical challenges, and develop a roadmap for further study.

## Lecture Schedule

| Week | Lecture | Notes | Slides |
|---|---|---|---|
| Week 1 | Lecture 01: Introduction: What is Physical AI? | Notes | [Slides](https://1drv.ms/p/c/831745eefddf18d9/IQDcJe5nNr1aT55wQnANUypHAR9lrb_BRc5bE60a1k5iy3M?e=qC3FmL) |
| Week 1 | Lecture 02: Anatomy of a Physical AI System |  |  |
| Week 2 | Lecture 03: Robot Bodies and Embodiment |  |  |
| Week 3 | Lecture 04: Sensors: How Robots Perceive the World |  |  |
| Week 4 | Lecture 05: Actuators: How Robots Move |  |  |
| Week 5-6 | Lecture 06: Basic Kinematics and Dynamics |  |  |
| Week 7 | Lecture 07: Perception and World Understanding |  |  |
| Week 8 | Lecture 08: From Goals to Actions: Planning and Policy |  |  |
| Week 9 | Lecture 09: Feedback Control |  |  |
| Weeks 10-11 | Lecture 10: Learning for Physical AI |  |  |
| Week 12 | Lecture 11: Physics for Physical AI |  |  |
| Week 13 | Lecture 12: Integrating Data and Physics |  |  |
| Week 14 | Lecture 13: Applications and Societal Impact |  |  |
| Weeks 15-16 | Lecture 14: Final Presentations / Future of Physical AI |  |  |

Full notes index: [Lecture Notes](notes/index.qmd)<br>
Full slides index: [Lecture Slides](slides/index.qmd)
